summary: MCPace now supports conservative automatic classification from indirect evidence plus a live safe-probe path for configured servers. It is ready to choose safe defaults automatically; it should only widen concurrency after probe evidence on the target machine.

implementedEvidenceSources.policyStrength: trusted static default plus post-install probe
candidate selection and launcher planning, not proof of safety
plan-only vs runnable, static-safe-policy vs needs-safe-probe
regression guard; catches broad-signal false positives

requiredForFullAutomation.description: mcpace lab probe / server test can start configured servers with the safe MCP lifecycle and request tools/list without tools/call. resources/list and prompts/list remain optional future enrichments.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Metadata-only left many random packages in needs-safe-probe or unknown-conservative; MCP lifecycle and tool discovery are the strongest non-destructive evidence.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: profileEvidence now carries evidenceScore, evidenceLevel, automaticAction and nextStep for dashboard/API consumers without adding brittle top-level fields.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Users need to see why MCPace chose static-safe-policy, needs-safe-probe, plan-only or blocked-high-risk.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: upstream policy audit now parses tool inputSchema/outputSchema words for path/file/sql/url/credential/command/mutation signals.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Tool names and descriptions are weaker than schemas; schemas reveal paths, queries, credentials, mutation payloads and destructive operations.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: Generate a reviewable permission manifest from env/header requirements, filesystem path flags, network domains, process/browser capabilities and tool effects.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Concurrency policy and security policy should be tied to the same evidence.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: Expand random sweeps beyond npm to PyPI, OCI/Docker, NuGet and MCPB metadata where available.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Current random-500 evidence is heavily npm-biased.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: Cache package version, dist integrity, registry metadata hash and tools/list fingerprint; downgrade policy when any of them changes.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: A server can change metadata or tool surface after approval.

requiredForFullAutomation.description: Use bounded concurrent metadata fetch with per-package timeout, cache and resume files for 500+ sweeps.
requiredForFullAutomation.why: Sequential npm pack/download was slow and sometimes hung in sandbox.

usableNow: mcpace auto can choose safe conservative defaults without asking the user for server type
weak/unknown random servers are routed to needs-safe-probe or plan-only instead of guessed policies
mcpace lab probe provides the live safe probe path for configured servers
notFinishedUntil: permission manifest inference is connected to auto-install review
drift/rug-pull detection downgrades policy when package or tools/list fingerprints change
Rust fmt/clippy/test/build pass in a Rust-capable environment
